description: First tagged desktop release v0.8.1 was cut via the new CI workflow and verified end-to-end: dreamcontext app install successfully pulled the asset from /releases/latest, verified the .sha256 checksum, passed codesign --verify --deep, and installed to ~/Applications with no Gatekeeper quarantine (ditto transfer preserves the ad-hoc signature). The continuous-app-update loop is now proven end-to-end. Desktop version 0.8.1 was chosen (not 0.8.0) to ensure the bundle version > last installed manifest version so the update loop does not trigger an infinite re-download cycle. CLI npm 0.8.x and desktop v0.8.x version numbers are coincidental, not coupled.
summary: First real desktop release v0.8.1 cut + E2E verified: app install pulled from /releases/latest, .sha256 verified, codesign --verify --deep passed, installed to ~/Applications with no quarantine

description: npm publish 0.8.0 went live on the latest tag. The release bundles: default-on CLI auto-upgrade (maybeTriggerUpgrade on session start), dreamcontext app install|update command, the continuous desktop update loop (maybeTriggerAppUpdate), self-contained CLI bundle (noExternal), and the desktop thin-shell. CLI and desktop version lines are INDEPENDENT — 0.8.x npm and v0.8.x desktop tags happen to coincide but are not coupled.
summary: npm 0.8.0 published to latest — auto-upgrade epic (default-on CLI upgrade + app command + continuous desktop update) live for users

description: The desktop app icon was updated to match the dreamcontext brand diamond — a white diamond on a squircle background. Tauri icon regeneration was run to produce all required platform sizes (32×32, 64×64, 128×128, 128×128@2x, Windows square tiles, Android mipmap sets, StoreLogo). No code changes; purely asset update.
summary: App icon fitted to brand diamond logo (white squircle); Tauri icons regenerated

description: The in-app onboarding wizard now exposes platform and skill-pack selection in both the new-project and existing-folder paths. Backend: ScaffoldAnswers gains platforms[] + packs[]; scaffoldProject normalizes platforms (defaults to ['claude']) and passes the list to init/setup; validates packs against the catalog and runs install-skill --packs --platforms after setup. New GET /api/launcher/catalog (vault-agnostic) serves the platform + pack choices. Strict-pick on the scaffold body: arrays filtered to strings; packs validated against known catalog names so no arbitrary args reach the CLI shell. Existing-folder flow routes through platform + skill-pack steps (STEPS_EXISTING_HASCTX path) so users can add platforms (e.g. Codex) and install skill packs on connect. The modal caps at viewport height (max-height: calc(100vh - space-8)); step content scrolls inside a .wiz-body region while close, error, and nav stay pinned. Pack/platform card descriptions clamped to 2 lines. 15 launcher unit tests + 1 integration green (6 new).
summary: Launcher wizard selects platforms (Claude recommended + Codex) and optional skill packs for both new and existing-folder flows; GET /api/launcher/catalog; wizard scrolls within viewport

description: After installing the CLI, on macOS (uname Darwin) install.sh now runs 'dreamcontext app install' so a fresh curl|sh setup also places the desktop app in ~/Applications. Best-effort: skipped silently if no desktop release is published yet or the download fails — never aborts the CLI install. Non-macOS platforms skip entirely. Opt out with DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP env var. The npm install -g path stays CLI-only by design; this app step is specific to the curl|sh installer.
summary: install.sh installs the desktop app on macOS (best-effort, opt-out via DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP)

description: Add .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml. On a v* tag push (or manual dispatch) it builds the dreamcontext-beta Tauri .app on macos-14 (aarch64), ad-hoc DEEP-signs it with entitlements (no Apple Developer ID / notarization), packages it as dreamcontext-beta_<ver>_aarch64.app.tar.gz plus a mandatory .sha256, verifies (extract + checksum -c + codesign --verify --deep), and publishes both to a non-draft GitHub Release. This is the factory + shelf the already-shipped client code (app install|update + maybeTriggerAppUpdate) needs: with a live release it can pull the arch-matching asset from /releases/latest, verify the checksum, and install via ditto (no quarantine bit, no Gatekeeper notarization) with zero further client changes.
summary: Release workflow for Tauri .app: v* tag → macos-14 build → ad-hoc deep-sign + entitlements → .tar.gz + .sha256 → GitHub Release

description: Migration 0.8.0 applied: scaffold-federation
summary: Scaffolded federation: state/.connections.json, state/.federation-inbox/, state/.federation-inbox/consumed/, state/.config.json (shareable:false)

description: Clean up desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs by removing the now-dead open_vault Tauri command and its supporting code: the Port struct + State import (no longer needed since vault opening is handled by the built-in WebviewWindow JS API), sanitize_label and urlencode helper functions, and the invoke_handler macro call. This code was deferred from the original Tauri beta task when the architecture decision was made to use built-in WebviewWindow rather than custom Rust commands (Tauri v2 ACL blocks custom commands on remote-origin pages). cargo check passes clean.
summary: Remove dead Rust code from lib.rs: open_vault command, Port state, sanitize_label, urlencode, invoke_handler, State import

description: Add src/lib/ensure-cli.ts with ensureCliInstalled(): best-effort, never-throws helper that probes command -v dreamcontext via the user's LOGIN shell ($SHELL -lc) so a Finder-launched Tauri app sees nvm/brew PATH entries that are absent from the default non-login env. When the CLI is missing it runs npm install -g dreamcontext@latest. The scaffold handler calls it post-scaffold and surfaces {cli:{status}} (present|installed|failed) in the response; the wizard success screen shows a relevant status message. Rationale: scaffolded projects install .claude/ hooks that call npx dreamcontext hook ... — if no global CLI exists npx would hang or fail silently in a non-interactive hook context. Four unit tests cover present/installed/npm-absent/install-failed paths. Full suite: 1784 green.
summary: Auto-provision global dreamcontext CLI on scaffold: login-shell probe + npm install -g, best-effort never-throws

description: Move detectTechStack from src/cli/commands/init.ts into a new standalone module src/lib/tech-stack.ts that accepts a directory parameter (defaults to cwd). Both the init command and the new launcher scaffold route consume the shared function. Previously the function lived inside the init command and was duplicated for the launcher; the extraction eliminates the duplication and makes tech-stack detection independently testable.
summary: Extract detectTechStack into src/lib/tech-stack.ts, shared by init and launcher scaffold

description: Add a full in-app onboarding flow to the dreamcontext-beta Launcher. Backend: two new vault-agnostic routes behind the existing CSRF guard — POST /api/launcher/scaffold (create-or-init-existing folder, then register vault; strict-pick body; path-traversal guarded via safeChildPath; idempotent when _dream_context/ already exists) and GET /api/launcher/detect (tech-stack + hasContext + basename for quiz prefill) — plus the existing GET /api/launcher/defaults (home + ~/projects). Scaffold runs the bundled CLI in a child process (execFile, no shell, explicit arg array, 120 s timeout) with cwd=target so the long-lived server's own cwd is never mutated: init --yes --name … --platforms claude then setup --defaults --platforms claude (init skipped on re-run). Frontend: new OnboardingWizard.tsx + CSS — a multi-step quiz (new vs existing; name+parentDir+Browse; description; target user; stack; priority; review; success screen) with a copyable 'hand off to Claude' enrichment prompt card and CLI-install status indicator. LauncherPage '+ Add Project' now opens the wizard. New hooks in useLauncher.ts: useScaffoldProject, probeFolder, useLauncherDefaults. The enrichment prompt is deterministic and LLM-free — quiz answers map directly to dreamcontext token-fill; the Claude hand-off card handles the rich initializer step post-scaffold.
summary: Quiz-style in-app project onboarding wizard: scaffold new/existing projects from the desktop Launcher without leaving the app

description: Enrich the dreamcontext-initializer agent so a fresh _dream_context/ starts with real content rather than empty placeholders. Feature detection derives a ranked candidate list from product surfaces (routes, modules, CLI, API) and scaffolds PRDs via 'features create --status planning'. Multi-people roster is seeded from git authors via a new 'config people <names...>' CLI command that writes the roster to .config.json AND syncs the ## People block in 1.user.md (implements the previously-unhooked ensurePeopleSection). Knowledge seeding distills README/docs/ADRs into 'knowledge create'. Tech stack and data-structures are populated from real inspection (actual schemas), fixing the stale core/data-structures path to knowledge/data-structures. A mandatory self-verification pass ensures no template tokens or placeholders ship. Optional planning warm-up via 'core releases add/active'. Agent file mirrored to .codex (toml regenerated + prompts copy). Integration test: tests/integration/config-people.test.ts covers roster write, dedup, idempotent user.md sync, --clear, empty-rejection.
summary: Richer bootstrap: feature detection, multi-people roster seeding, knowledge distillation, real tech_stack + data-structures, self-verification pass (#10, PR #10)

description: Add discoverVaultsAsync (src/lib/vault-discovery.ts) as an async companion to the existing synchronous discoverVaults. Uses fast-glob's async API so a large directory tree scan does not block the Node event loop. The launcher server route (src/server/routes/launcher.ts) uses the async variant so a slow home-directory scan does not freeze the dashboard while it awaits the vault list.
summary: discoverVaultsAsync: non-blocking async variant of vault discovery to avoid wedging the single-thread server

description: Switch tsup.config.ts from externalizing runtime dependencies to bundling them directly into dist/index.js via noExternal. The Tauri .app ships the dist/ directory but not node_modules, so previously externalized deps (commander, chalk, gray-matter, @inquirer/prompts, fast-glob, nanoid, boxen) would fail with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime inside the app bundle. A banner shim at the top of the output file provides a createRequire implementation so bundled CJS dependencies that call require() for Node builtins continue to work under ESM output. The resulting dist/index.js is ~2.1MB and fully self-contained. This change affects the npm package itself: the published dist/index.js now embeds its dependencies rather than declaring them as peer runtime requirements; package.json dependencies remain listed for compatibility but are no longer needed at runtime on the consumer's machine.
summary: Self-contained CLI bundle: noExternal deps in tsup + createRequire shim so dist/index.js ships without node_modules (needed for Tauri .app)

description: Add a Tauri 2 macOS native desktop wrapper for the existing React+Node dashboard, installed to /Applications/dreamcontext-beta.app and shipped as a signed ad-hoc .dmg (desktop/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/dmg/dreamcontext-beta_0.8.0_aarch64.dmg, ~20MB). The app runs in 'launcher mode' (contextRoot=null on startup): a LauncherPage lists all registered vaults from the global ~/.dreamcontext/vaults.json registry; clicking 'Open' spawns a new native window via the built-in Tauri WebviewWindow API (not a custom command — see ACL decision below) pointed at /?vault=<name>, which resolves to an X-Dreamcontext-Vault request header via server middleware, then a strict name-only contextRoot resolver on each request. This architecture means one shared Node server handles all vaults, with no inter-window data leakage because every request carries its own vault binding. The app is non-sandboxed with custom entitlements and Info.plist TCC usage strings to allow local filesystem access. Architecture knowledge doc already written at _dream_context/knowledge/desktop-beta-tauri-multivault.md. Stale /Applications/dreamcontext.app (v0.6) removed during install. Note: local beta only — not code-signed by Apple, not notarized, not on auto-updater.
summary: dreamcontext-beta Tauri 2 macOS app: multi-vault launcher with one shared Node server and per-window vault headers (local, unsigned)

description: Add local cross-project context federation across three phases, merged via PR #31 (merge commit a34c1f8). Phase 1 (read-only): 'vaults discover [root] [--register]' scans a directory tree for _dream_context/ projects and writes a global vault registry; 'memory recall --vault/--connected/--all-vaults' queries across registered vaults with <vault>::<type>/<slug> namespacing; 'snapshot --vault' generates a context snapshot for any registered vault; 'config shareable on|off' is a private-by-default cross-vault read gate; federated docs (CorpusDoc.federated) are excluded from cross-vault serving to prevent transitive leaks. Phase 2 (connections + dashboard): state/.connections.json with direction (out/in/both) + topics + consent semantics; 'connect/disconnect/connections list' CLI; GET /api/vaults, GET/POST/DELETE /api/connections server routes with strict-pick and direction enum validation; dashboard Connections manager UI with direction toggle and shareable switch. Phase 3 (sleep-driven digest inbox): relevance-filtered digest push into state/.federation-inbox/ during sleep; filename-based dedup with safeChildPath-guarded writes; slug-collision namespacing; conflict bookmarks; 'federation drain/sync/status' CLI; a dedicated sleep-federation specialist (agents/sleep-federation.md); GET /api/federation/inbox + POST /api/federation/sync (dry-run by construction) server routes; dashboard inbox preview. Includes 0.8.0 versioned migration scaffolding (connections/, inbox/, shareable init). Four independent security reviewers confirmed: hot path (no-arg generateSnapshot) untouched, transitive-leak guard holds, SSRF/path-traversal blocked at two independent layers, prototype-pollution-safe request bodies, migration idempotency confirmed. 1770 tests pass (1 pre-existing skip). Closes #25.
summary: Local cross-project context mesh: vaults discover, cross-vault recall, connections, sleep-driven digest inbox, 0.8.0 migration (#25, PR #31)

description: Add a full-screen mode to the knowledge document viewer (#21, PR #30). New generic FullscreenOverlay component (dashboard/src/components/layout/FullscreenOverlay.tsx) is a fixed in-app dialog — not the browser Fullscreen API — with role=dialog + aria-modal, focus moved in and trapped (selector excludes disabled elements via :not(:disabled)), focus returned to the trigger on close, Esc handled via a document-capture keydown listener, and body scroll locked with scrollbar-width compensation so there is no layout shift. KnowledgePage gains a ⛶ expand button next to the File/Preview tabs for markdown, SQL-ER, excalidraw, and raw views; tab state is shared with the pane so it survives entering/leaving full-screen, and pane content unmounts while the overlay is open so excalidraw/mermaid never render the same doc twice (ExcalidrawPreview's mount-time fit + ResizeObserver re-fit the board to the larger canvas). Playwright spec e2e/knowledge-fullscreen.spec.ts covers open/close paths, tabs inside the overlay, scroll lock, list/search preservation, and that the excalidraw svg actually grows. Post-review hardening (0b0c2ac) fixed four findings: focus/Esc escape via the document capture listener, the :not(:disabled) focus-trap selector, an :only-child CSS specificity fix, and a robust e2e excalidraw fit assertion. Closes #21.
summary: Full-screen mode for the knowledge document viewer via a generic FullscreenOverlay; covers markdown/SQL-ER/excalidraw/raw views (#21)

description: Enforce the owner product rule that unplanned tasks are tagged 'backlog' and MUST NOT carry a due date. Enforcement lives at the backend level (normalizeBacklogFields in src/lib/task-backend/local.ts) so it holds across every surface: tagging a task 'backlog' clears its due_date; explicitly dating a task removes the backlog tag. The rule applies uniformly to CLI, dashboard, and ClickUp sync, and is pinned in the TaskBackend conformance suite so future backends inherit it.
summary: Product rule: backlog-tagged tasks are undated on every surface — backlog tag clears due_date, dating a task drops the backlog tag (#11, owner decision)

description: Ship the pluggable TaskBackend architecture end-to-end (#11, merged via PR #27). M1 extracted local file ops behind a provider-agnostic TaskBackend interface with golden byte-identity fixtures and a conformance suite; M2 added the identity layer, ClickUp config + token storage (secrets.ts), and a generic ApiAdapter; M3 the ClickUp backend with watermark-based PUSH via mocked-transport conformance; M4 delta PULL + two-way merge engine + conflicts + WAL replay (client-side watermark guard since ClickUp date_updated_gt is >=); M5 git/post-sleep sync triggers, config surfaces, dashboard Settings + sync status, docs/clickup.md. Follow-ups hardened it to GA: custom-field bridge (urgency/summary/RICE/feature via list custom fields), member discovery + person-tag/assignee bridge (one concept across CLI and dashboard UI), due_date end-to-end, 'tasks provision' (create + backfill recommended remote fields), deletion everywhere + remote-to-local deletion reconciliation with request-budget discipline, remote-changes journal into sleep, ascii-folded status matching against the list's actual status set (real lists have statuses like 'in revıew'), a per-project sync lock (one sync engine), stale-server detection (capabilities in /api/health + restart banner), polished guided onboarding (list picker from API, connection test, provision + first-sync offers, list migration, hook cleanup), and a dashboard Sync button for remote backends. Cloud task management stays an Advanced opt-in setting. Closes #11.
summary: Pluggable TaskBackend GA: ClickUp remote backend behind a generic adapter — two-way sync, conflicts, custom-field bridge, guided onboarding (#11)

description: Add a faceted controlled tag vocabulary (domain/layer/kind/topic) as enforced behavior — taxonomy quality drives recall quality since tags are weighted 2x in BM25 and are first-class graph nodes. src/lib/taxonomy.ts provides alias resolution, normalization, near-duplicate detection (Levenshtein <=1 same-facet), array-union merge of project vocab over defaults, and a report-only corpus audit. Storage is core/taxonomy.json (matches CHANGELOG.json/RELEASES.json pattern) with fail-soft parse and inherited-object-key hardening. Mutations go through validated CLI only: 'taxonomy add' (facet whitelist, reuse-before-invent), 'taxonomy alias' (no chains/cycles), 'taxonomy resolve', 'vocab --facet'; sleep agents and the initializer are instructed to mutate only via these commands. Recall indexes faceted tags by value only (facet prefixes never become high-df noise tokens) and expands queries through project alias groups opt-in. Dashboard gains a Taxonomy page (facet chip clusters with usage counts, alias arrows, drift/audit panel) backed by read-only GET /api/taxonomy. SessionStart hook seeds core/taxonomy.json crash-safely on pre-feature installs. SKILL.md gains the 'Tag before you create.' rule; sleep-product owns taxonomy maintenance, sleep-tasks normalizes task tags, sleep-state flags drift.
summary: Faceted tag taxonomy as enforced behavior: core/taxonomy.json storage, validated CLI mutations, dashboard Taxonomy page, SessionStart auto-seed

description: Make .excalidraw.md boards first-class knowledge. Previously a board's entire embedded scene JSON (a real board is 2.2MB) was tokenized into the BM25 recall corpus, stored as knowledge-index content, and inlined into snapshots — poisoning recall and bloating the index. New src/lib/excalidraw-text.ts extractExcalidrawText keeps frontmatter + '## Text Elements' labels and strips the Drawing block (json and compressed-json), Embedded Files map, Obsidian banner, and ^blockref suffixes, with a 200-line cap; malformed/empty boards fall back to frontmatter-only, never raw body. Extraction is applied in both knowledge-index.ts (entry content, snapshot warm/pinned) and recall.ts (BM25 corpus, reflection); the dashboard keeps rendering the full scene. The diagrams migration is genuinely runnable, crash-safe, and path-contained, with clarified board placement under knowledge/diagrams/. Closes #20.
summary: Excalidraw boards as first-class knowledge: index extracted text only, never scene JSON; runnable diagrams migration (#20)

description: Replace hand-baked unconditional migration calls in 'sleep start' with a versioned, recorded, forward-only migration system so structural brain changes migrate on update/sleep, are tracked per-clone, and surface to the user. Registry at src/migrations/ holds one module per version transition (0.7.0.ts wraps the data-structures move + SQL-fence backfill); pendingMigrations((from,to]) returns semver-ordered transitions. Ledger at state/.migrations.json records {version,step,executor,timestamp,filesTouched,summary} with atomic tmp+rename writes; team clones with already-migrated content get a 'detected' backfill touching zero files, and code steps stay filesystem-idempotent (the ledger gate is an optimization, not the safety net). migration-runner.ts runs on 'dreamcontext update' and as a 'sleep start' safety net; downgrades run nothing. Applied-step notices queue into .sleep.json and surface as a one-line snapshot note. New agents/sleep-migration.md handles STRUCTURE-only migrations with a 'dreamcontext migrations pending|record' CLI. Post-review fixes: ledger dedup, fence-safe wikilink rewrite (wikilink-rewrite.ts skips code fences, preserves alias/anchor), sanitized migration note. Closes #23.
summary: Versioned migration system: registry + per-clone ledger + sleep-migration agent; runs on update and sleep start (#23)

description: Fix CLI upgrades silently leaving consumer projects on stale skills/agents/hooks: setupVersion was write-only and never compared against the running CLI. 'dreamcontext update' now bumps setupVersion on success (when core assets refresh) and prints a relay-able refreshed/pruned summary. New pure src/lib/setup-drift.ts computes drift state (current/stale/bootstrap/downgrade/disabled) and renders a SessionStart self-heal directive injected into the snapshot's never-evict tier: stale prompts 'dreamcontext update' (content-safe), downgrade warns only, 0.0.0 gets a softer bootstrap variant. Hardened post-review: setupVersion/cliVersion values are sanitized (newlines + markdown-structural chars stripped, length-capped) before interpolation so a crafted value cannot inject a heading or instruction into agent context. Opt-out via DREAMCONTEXT_DRIFT_CHECK=0. Closes #22.
summary: Detect setupVersion drift and self-heal stale project assets via a SessionStart directive (#22)

description: Capture issue #19 as a test-first stub spec before implementation. dreamcontext should coexist with Graphify (code-structure graph tool) without building or indexing the graph itself. When `graphify-out/graph.json` (or root-level `graph.json`) is detected, the snapshot gains a one-line code-graph nudge; `doctor` prints an info line; the explore agent prompt is updated to route structure questions to the graph. Decision: do NOT build/refresh the graph ourselves (mem0 lesson), do NOT mix into BM25 corpus (hub docs hijack proven by linkAware rejection in recall v3). 12 `it.todo` tests in tests/unit/graphify-coexistence.test.ts cover the full contract. Implementation: create `src/lib/code-graph.ts` `hasCodeGraph()` and wire snapshot + doctor.
summary: Test-first spec for graphify coexistence (#19): read-only detection, snapshot nudge, doctor info

description: Several dashboard UX improvements shipped this cycle. Page-level `<h1 className="page-title">` headings removed from Tasks, Features, and Knowledge pages (they ate viewport and duplicated sidebar nav). SleepPage gets a max-width alignment fix matching the rest of the app. FeaturesPage gains a client-side search input that filters features by name/description and auto-expands all folders while a query is active. KnowledgePage search similarly auto-expands entries. A new `ExcalidrawPreview` component (+ `dashboard/src/lib/excalidraw.ts`) renders Obsidian Excalidraw `.excalidraw.md` boards as static SVGs with pan/zoom-to-fit using panzoom, lazy-loading `@excalidraw/excalidraw` only when an Excalidraw knowledge file is opened. I18n strings added for features.search.
summary: Dashboard UX: page-title removal, Sleep page width fix, Features search, ExcalidrawPreview

description: Add agent-driven feedback loop so a dreamcontext-powered agent can take responsibility for reporting gaps it hits (unseen memory, missing CLI command, bug, friction) by filing a structured issue against the upstream `meanllbrl/dreamcontext` repo (#4). Core: `src/lib/feedback.ts` handles `gh` detection (installed/authenticated/account), issue body templating (Scenario / Expected / Gap / Repro / Suggested improvement / Environment), Jaccard-similarity dedup, and `gh issue create` wrapping. CLI: `dreamcontext feedback --title --category --scenario --dry-run --yes` with interactive draft preview and explicit confirmation before filing; if `gh` is absent/unauthenticated, guides the user through setup including `github.com/signup` for account-less users. Behavioral: SKILL.md gains an "Improving dreamcontext" section establishing the agent as field reporter with a draft-confirm-file loop and an explicit prohibition on silent workarounds. 17 unit tests. Closes #4.
summary: Agent feedback loop: `dreamcontext feedback` files structured GitHub issues upstream

description: Document file layout conventions for the excalidraw skill in SKILL.md (#16 follow-up). Covers three patterns: single board (spec next to generated .excalidraw.md in a `_spec/` subfolder), multi-board pipeline (`boards/pipeline/spec/` with a single `generate.js` entrypoint that rebuilds all boards), and many-boards-from-shared-data (one board per item from a shared JSON data set). Establishes the convention that generated `.excalidraw.md` files are disposable derivations of their specs, and that the `ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '..')` pattern keeps generators portable regardless of invocation directory. Two Excalidraw diagrams committed to `_dream_context/knowledge/diagrams/` as living examples.
summary: Excalidraw skill: spec/output file layout conventions for single-board and pipeline patterns

description: Implement AI-driven multi-person detection and attribution (#8). The sleep cycle detects multiple distinct human git authors using a two-signal corroboration gate (distinct `git log --format='%an'` authors + self-identification in session transcripts). On detection, the people roster is appended to `state/.config.json` (additive union, never overwrite), a `## People` section is added/refreshed in `1.user.md`, and changelog entries for that cycle are attributed via `--authors` slugs. Single-person projects produce byte-identical output. Detection logic lives in sleep-state Pass B.5; all schema additions are backwards-compatible. 21 stub tests in tests/unit/multi-people-awareness.test.ts cover the full contract. Closes #8.
summary: Multi-people awareness: auto-detect collaborators, attribute changelog/tasks per person

description: Add freshness tracking and validation to the features system. Each feature PRD gains an `updated` frontmatter field; `features set` can stamp it without hand-editing. `dreamcontext features doctor` runs a validation pass: warns on PRDs not updated in 30+ days, flags missing `why` sections, checks that frontmatter fields are valid, and reports PRDs linked from tasks that don't exist. The sleep-product specialist now runs `features doctor` as part of its pass and surfaces staleness counts in its report. Closes #13.
summary: PRD freshness tracking + `features doctor` warns on stale/missing PRDs

description: Add SQL fence rendering for knowledge/data-structures/ files. The dashboard MarkdownPreview detects a single ```sql fenced block and renders it with highlight.js SQL syntax highlighting using the brand palette (issue #12 follow-up). An idempotent backfill migration wraps already-migrated data-structure files that lacked the fence: runs on `sleep start` so no manual migration step is needed. `dreamcontext init` now scaffolds new data-structure files with the fenced template. Closes the round trip opened by #12 — all data-structure files now have consistent, highlighted bodies regardless of when they were migrated.
summary: Render data-structures as fenced SQL; backfill existing files on sleep start
Snapshot budget ladder: 20K→10.4K tokens, brain loads inline again
description: Snapshot token budget + demotion ladder shipped: sections demote through curated renders (memory decisions→titles, tasks top-N sorted by activity, features detail→name-lines, knowledge index→compact slugs, warm capped) — never raw truncation, never-evict identity tier, recovery footer. Default 10K tokens via DREAMCONTEXT_SNAPSHOT_BUDGET (0/off disables). Live: 20,253→10,386 tokens, hook output 79.2KB→42KB (was being persisted with 2KB blind preview — brain not loading). PreCompact now digests the live transcript (partial:true, superseded by full digest at catch-up) so compacted-away decisions are recallable on the next prompt. sleep-tasks gained a 21-day staleness sweep.

summary: Recall v3: TR+EN uplift, held-out validated, no regressions
description: Recall engine v3: TR morphology (two-hop suffix folding, possessive compounds, question-word stopwords), directed synonym bridges (paraphrase→canonical one-way), EN -e fold fix (databases↔database families now merge), CHANGELOG_RANK_FACTOR=0.85 canonical-first. Train r@1 86.7→91.7, blind held-out r@1 83.3→93.3, TR held-out r@1 70→90 r@3 80→100. Zero category regressions on either set. linkAware tested and REJECTED with data (overall r@1 craters to 68.3 — hub docs hijack). Held-out set: eval/gold-heldout.jsonl (30q, authored blind).

Version bump to v0.7.0 to align npm package with post-v0.6.0-tag commits. Covers: data-structures migration to knowledge/, SQL fence backfill on sleep start, tasks list --tag/--version/--group-by filters, features doctor freshness tracking, multi-people attribution, Eisenhower matrix drag-and-drop, video-watching VFR-robust sampling.
summary: v0.7.0: data-structures→knowledge migration, SQL fence backfill, tasks filter/group, features doctor, multi-people awareness, Eisenhower D&D, video VFR fix

description: Fix systematic under-sampling of UI/app screen recordings in the video-watching skill pack. Static screens that change by text only do not trip scene-detect, and the previous 10s gap-fill default silently dropped most of them, causing downstream teardowns to wrongly report screens as not shown. Replace the brittle two-pass scene-detect plus per-frame -ss grab pipeline with a single VFR-robust ffmpeg filter pass: `select='eq(n,0)+gt(scene,TH)+gte(t-prev_selected_t,MAX_GAP)'`. Retires gap_fill.py. New flags: --frames-only (skip whisper), --mode ui|lecture (sets MAX_GAP to 2.5s vs 10s), --max-gap, --scene-threshold, --contact-sheet, --lang. Coverage QA in build_frame_index.py prints the longest unsampled gap, warns on gaps exceeding 2x MAX_GAP, and reconstructs scene/gap type from frame spacing. Multi-review fixes folded in: fail loudly on 0-frame extraction, dedupe the t=0 seed against anchor_first, surface the tail gap when duration is unknown, and validate numeric knobs (--max-gap > 0). Adds tests/unit/video-frame-index.test.ts. Closes #15.
summary: Time-based VFR-robust frame sampling + --frames-only/--mode ui|lecture flags; fail loudly on 0-frame extraction

description: Add drag-and-drop reprioritization to the Eisenhower matrix view. Dropping a task into a quadrant applies the minimal priority/urgency change needed to land it in that quadrant's high/not-high bucket, preserving within-bucket granularity (a critical task stays critical, not demoted to high). Three interactive affordances added, all functional with no decorative overlay: a live drop-preview chip showing the exact priority/urgency change a drop will apply (or 'Already here' for a no-op drop into the source quadrant); a single-level undo bar that restores pre-move state after any move; and source/target drag affordances plus a dimmed ghost on the dragged card. The pure quadrant and bucket-mapping logic is extracted into a React-free eisenhower.ts module covered by 18 unit tests (vitest). Closes #7.
summary: Eisenhower matrix drag-and-drop: live drop preview, single-level undo, pure eisenhower.ts logic module

description: Address two findings surfaced by multi-review of #5 and #6. In markdown.ts, `isPlaceholderLine` now only strips parentheticals that begin with a recognized template-filler stem, so a real one-line note like `- (see RFC-42)` is no longer silently wiped by stripPlaceholderBody (data-loss guard). In task-query.ts, groupTasks/collectTags sort with localeCompare(_, 'en', {sensitivity:'base'}) so mixed-case tag and version order is deterministic across CI and dev machines rather than dependent on system locale. Regression tests added for both.
summary: Multi-review guard fixes: placeholder-stripping data-loss guard + locale-stable tag/version sort

description: Relocate data-structures from core/ to knowledge/data-structures/<product>.md so they are recall-indexed, staleness-tracked, and ownership moves to sleep-product. An idempotent migration runs on `sleep start`, leaving the old directory in place with a pointer; `doctor` validates the new path and nags on legacy locations. knowledge-index.ts now recurses subdirectories and emits subdir-qualified slugs (data-structures/lina, products/lina) so the Knowledge view and snapshot surface all entries. The server router gains `*param` rest-route support so subdir slugs resolve on GET/PATCH (previously the preview pane was blank for any subdir slug). Dashboard MarkdownPreview adds highlight.js syntax highlighting with theme-aware brand palette, running post-DOMPurify on in-DOM blocks (SQL prioritized). Agent prompt files and .codex mirrors updated to reflect sleep-product ownership. Skill docs updated. 14 unit tests (migration, index recursion, router) + integration coverage. Closes #12.
summary: Move data-structures into knowledge/data-structures/, add SQL syntax highlighting in dashboard, subdir-slug router fix

description: Overhaul features authoring ergonomics. Insert now replaces template placeholders on first write and keeps a blank line before the next ## header, so re-running insert never produces double-blank or orphaned placeholder lines. user_stories and acceptance_criteria sections auto-format entries as `- [ ]` items via a shared section-insert.ts library (tasks insert benefits too). `features create` gains --tags, --status, --related-tasks flags; a new `features set` command patches frontmatter fields without hand-editing. All interactive prompts (tasks complete, insert, log, features create/insert) now fall through to sensible defaults in non-TTY environments (agent/CI runs), eliminating hangs. 19 unit + 7 integration tests. Closes #6.
summary: Features authoring DX: non-lossy section insert, frontmatter flags via CLI, non-TTY safe prompts

description: Add rich filtering and grouping to `tasks list`. New flags: --tag (repeatable, AND), --any-tag (repeatable, OR), --version, --priority, --feature for filtering; --group-by tag|version|priority|status for sectioned output with per-group counts; --long to show version+tags inline; --json for scriptable output; and a new `tasks tags` subcommand that shows distinct tags with counts. The filtering logic lives in a pure task-query.ts library (no Commander coupling) so it is directly testable. Also fixes a root-level Commander .version() call that shadowed every subcommand's --version flag. 26 unit tests + 8 integration tests. Closes #5.
summary: Add filter+group to `tasks list`: --tag/--any-tag/--version/--priority/--feature/--group-by/--long/--json + `tasks tags`

description: Add three test-first spec files under tests/unit/ as part of a multi-improvement initiative. Each improvement is captured as a GitHub issue plus a stub spec before implementation begins. multi-people-awareness.test.ts (21 todo) pins issue #8 (auto-detect multiple humans, attribute work across changelog/tasks/memory/user profile). sleep-system-360.test.ts (3 real + 23 todo) pins issue #9 (360 degree sleep/consolidation review); key finding: sleep lifecycle invariants such as epoch-safe clearing, double-count prevention, and debt-level thresholds live inline inside sleep done's .action() handler and private helpers in src/cli/commands/sleep.ts, making them untestable today without extraction to pure exported functions. task-backend.test.ts (31 todo) pins issue #11 (pluggable task backend: optional GitHub Projects source, sync model with gitignored local mirror, write-through, and offline WAL). GitHub issues #8, #9, #10 (initializer improvements), and #11 were all opened this session. A 'sleep-rules override file' concept was discussed and deferred: existing CLAUDE.md and soul-level customization plus per-run user hint already cover orchestration-level sleep customization.

Standalone init now detects a half-installed state (scaffolded _dream_context/ but no .claude/ skills/agents/hooks) by checking integrationPresent across ALL selected platforms (.every(), not .some()) and either offers to finish or emits a loud warning: '⚠ Not done yet → dreamcontext setup'. A user following the obvious path can no longer end up in a broken half-install.

description: Major polish pass on the About/landing page. FlowDiagram caption text now auto-wraps via SVG foreignObject instead of overflowing boxes. Flow animation engine replaced: dropped stroke-dashoffset (re-rasterizes the full path stroke on main-thread every frame, causing visible stutter) in favor of CSS Motion Path (offset-path + offset-distance), which compositor-class animates a small dot element with a radial-gradient fill; worst frame measured at 9-11ms, 0 jank at ~27 dots. Goal-skill promoted to the 'One brain, many faculties' feature spotlight with a dedicated landscape loop diagram showing Plan Review → Implementation Review → Validation Review cycle. The section itself was refactored from a flat scroll to a sticky + scroll-scrub experience: a sentinel element triggers a sticky container while the user scrolls through sub-scenes, with snap-to-nearest so the user never ends up in an in-between state. Light-mode wire contrast fixed: FlowDiagram.css .fd-wire color changed from var(--color-border) (near-white in light) to color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-text) 38%, transparent), making wires visible in both themes without overpowering dark mode. Dashed-loop pattern tightened from 2 8 to 5 7 + opacity 0.85 for legibility at small sizes. All changes in dashboard/src/components/about/* and AboutPage.css; uncommitted in working tree as of this sleep.
summary: Landing page v2 polish: motion-path flow animation, goal-skill spotlight, sticky scrub section, wire contrast fix

description: Rewrite Step 2 of sleep-tasks.md to enforce a mandatory recall-before-create dedup gate: recall --types task + active-list scan before opening any new task. Decision table: smaller slice/sub-step/follow-up → fold into existing task (broaden description: + Why, insert user_stories|acceptance_criteria|notes); same work re-observed → update existing; genuinely separate work → new task. Add Rule #1 and update the report example. Rewrite B2 of sleep-product.md with a sharp-vs-soft-boundary knowledge consolidation rubric: soft distinction (same vertical/brand/topic family, incremental finding) → extend existing file; sharp distinction (distinct topic where separate tags add precision) → new file. Add Rule #8 and update the report example. Add a one-line 'Consolidation discipline' note to the sleep flow in skill/SKILL.md so the orchestrator brief carries the principle. All four canonical locations updated (agents/, .codex/agents/prompts/) and dist/ rebuilt.
summary: Harden sleep-tasks + sleep-product dedup rules: recall-before-create + consolidation rubric

The page is composed of 9 sections: Hero, ProblemSection, HowItWorksSection, FeaturesShowcase, ArchitectureSection, RecallFlowSection, SleepFlowSection, SkillPacksMarquee, ClosingSection. Fix a latent SVG gradient-collision bug where multiple FlowDiagram instances shared the same gradient def IDs, causing colors to bleed across diagrams; fix scopes IDs with a per-instance uid. description: Add src/cli/commands/reflect.ts and src/lib/reflection.ts. The reflect command scans session digests and bookmarks for recurring terms not yet in curated knowledge/features/memory, surfaces them as candidate generalizations in state/.reflection.md (capped at 8KB). Constraints baked in: detectPatterns and formatReflection are pure (no fs calls); writeReflection writes only state/.reflection.md; no fetch/spawn/LLM/network calls in the module. IDF is computed from the recall corpus via buildCorpus; DF counter uses sessions as documents (Set<sessionKey>). Only terms meeting DEFAULT_MIN_SESSIONS=3 and not in REFLECTION_NOISE pass. Registered in src/cli/index.ts.
summary: Add reflection engine: deterministic cross-session pattern detection writing state/.reflection.md

description: Add src/cli/commands/config.ts and src/lib/claude-settings.ts implementing 'dreamcontext config native-memory enable|disable'. The command writes autoMemoryEnabled:true/false into .claude/settings.json (preserving all other keys), idempotent. Rationale: Claude Code v2.1.59+ auto-loads/writes a MEMORY.md per project; the official kill-switch is autoMemoryEnabled:false. dreamcontext disables it by default on setup so it is the single source of project memory — two competing memory systems dilute recall. User correction: the official key is autoMemoryEnabled (not memoryEnabled or native_memory). Module is server-safe (no interactive deps). Registered in src/cli/index.ts; documented in SKILL.md command reference.
summary: Add 'config native-memory enable|disable' command; disable Claude auto-memory on setup by default

description: Add two new standalone skill-packs: video-watching (SKILL.md + scripts) and excalidraw (SKILL.md + scripts + examples + reference + package.json). Introduce the bundleDir field on CatalogStandalone entries so that cpSync copies the full directory (binaries, scripts, exec bits preserved) rather than a single SKILL.md file. Pin CJS subtrees with package.json {"type":"commonjs"} under the ESM host to prevent module resolution failures when scripts use require(). Exec bits are preserved on install. Both skills are listed in skill-packs/catalog.json. src/lib/install-packs.ts updated to handle bundleDir via cpSync.
summary: Vendor excalidraw + video-watching skills; add bundleDir install mechanism for code-bearing skill-packs

description: Two shipped together. (1) Continuous capture (Batch 2): session-digest.ts auto-digests transcripts on the SessionStart catch-up path (never Stop to avoid latency); salience.ts detects high-signal moments and creates bookmarks automatically; digests + bookmarks are indexed into the recall corpus so future sessions can surface them; recall hits bump knowledge_access for staleness tracking. Explore agent Bash allowlist now includes 'dreamcontext memory recall' + 'transcript distill' (Batch 3: resolves a self-contradiction that made context-first explore non-functional); dead marketing/.env PreToolUse write-block revived (was registered with matcher:'Agent' only). (2) Capture guard (PR #2, commit 4585612): measured that a flood of auto-captures degraded recall@3 −3.3% and recall@1 −8.3%. Fix: CAPTURE_RANK_PENALTY=0.5 applied to rankScore only (does not affect raw BM25 hit.score or gate thresholds), MAX_INDEXED_DIGESTS=50 hard cap; proven 0 true displacement at N=50/100/200. Also fixed a latent created_at Date-vs-string parse bug that silently disabled digest recency scoring.
summary: Continuous capture (auto-digest + salience detection + recall indexing) with capture guard (CAPTURE_RANK_PENALTY=0.5, MAX_INDEXED_DIGESTS=50)

description: Full recall-engine rewrite across src/lib/recall.ts, recall-synonyms.ts, and recall-query-extractor.ts. Replaces flat keyword scoring with BM25F field weighting (title×3, tags×2, description×2, body×1). Adds TR suffix folding + EN snowball stemming (eval-driven, no external lib) and a query-time synonym map (recall-synonyms.ts). Haiku relevance-ranking replaces the 8000-char positional corpus slice — Haiku now receives the full index and returns ranked doc keys rather than raw BM25 order. Critical architectural decision: all new signals (field weighting, recency, status, synonyms, link-aware, capture penalty) populate a DERIVED rankScore; hit.score stays flat BM25 so gate thresholds (BM25 fallback ≥2.0, skill gate ≥1.0, explore agent ≥5/<2) are completely unaffected by rank reordering. Phase-5 also changed the BM25-fallback gate from hits[0].score to hits.some(h=>h.score>=2.0) to prevent a strong raw match from being suppressed by reordering. Gold-set results (deterministic, 60 queries): overall recall@1 68.3→85.0%, recall@3 81.7→95.0%; Turkish 37.5→75.0%, paraphrase 41.7→66.7%; no category regressed. Eval harness at eval/ (gold.jsonl, harness.ts, BASELINE.md, RESULTS.md) — not shipped in npm package. 1063 tests pass.
summary: Recall engine overhaul: BM25F field weighting + TR/EN stemming + synonyms + Haiku rerank + score/rank decoupling (recall@1 68→85%, recall@3 82→95%)

description: Fix a critical bug in update.ts where the manifest-diff prune step could delete user-created files that dreamcontext never installed. The fix tightens isSafeDeletePath() to only remove files that appear in the install manifest with kind 'system'; any file absent from the manifest, or present with kind 'user', is now exempt from prune. Proven safe on a live repo run. Fixes the trust regression introduced when manifest-aware update shipped (v0.4.0).
summary: v0.5.4 — update never deletes user files it didn't install

description: Updated 17 failing test fixtures across tests/integration/hook.test.ts, tests/integration/sleep.test.ts, tests/integration/snapshot.test.ts, and tests/unit/sleep-state.test.ts to match current system behavior: recall_mode defaults to 'haiku' (not 'bm25'), pinned-by-reference knowledge files are indexed (not inlined), changelog tiering now uses 'type' field, and the sleep-state block is no longer emitted in the snapshot output. No source changes — fixture-only alignment.

description: Two changes in commit 62768a1. First: the UserPromptSubmit context gate now surfaces the FULL installed skill catalog rather than pre-selecting a top-3 relevant subset. The old design arbitrarily constrained which skills the agent considered; the new gate tells the agent to review all skills already in context and invoke any that fit. Internal BM25 scoring still determines WHETHER to fire the gate but never which skills to list. Tests updated: 'fires gate / review full list / no listing' assertion green. Second: 5 Codex review agents added to .codex/agents/ (review-cloud-functions, review-edge-cases, review-frontend, review-router, review-security) mirroring the multi-review skill specialist roster for Codex CLI users. This supersedes the earlier context-gate entry (2026-05-31|hook) which described the same redesign from a different session angle.
summary: Context gate surfaces full skill catalog (drop top-3 pre-pick); add 5 Codex review agents

description: Rewrote README.md and DEEP-DIVE.md to reflect the v0.5.x reality. README: new 'Skills' section explaining skill packs + goal-skill orchestration; expanded 'Staying Up to Date' with upgrade command + in-session nudge; corrected stale command examples and hook counts. DEEP-DIVE: added 'Install & Update' walkthrough (curl|sh idempotent install, upgrade check, CDN-install safety rationale) and 'Dashboard Security Model' section (loopback bind, CSRF guard, CORS lockdown, path-traversal guard). All 16 positioning-doc tests pass; banned 'autonomous' confirmed absent.
summary: Refresh README + DEEP-DIVE for v0.5.x: Skills section, update flow, dashboard security model, stale facts fixed

description: The UserPromptSubmit context gate iterated four times in a single session before landing on this design. The old gate identified a top-3 relevant skill subset and listed them by name — an arbitrary pre-selection that constrained which skills the agent considered. The new gate removes that list entirely and instead tells the agent: 'review the skills available to you (the full list is already in your context) and INVOKE any that fit this task via the Skill tool — do NOT limit yourself to a pre-selected few; scan them all and decide.' The full skill catalog is already injected by the harness on every session start, so the agent chooses from the complete set. The BM25 skill-relevance check is retained internally to decide WHETHER to fire the gate at all (suppressed for greetings and unrelated prompts) but never used to narrow WHICH skills the agent considers. Tests/integration/hook.test.ts updated; 17 user-prompt-submit tests green including 'fires gate / review full list / no listing' assertion.
summary: Redesign user-prompt-submit context gate: drop pre-selected top-3 skills, tell agent to review full catalog and decide

description: Security fast-follow for v0.5.0 dashboard server, pulled forward from v0.6 task after security review flagged the live attack surface before npm publish. Four fixes: (1) server.listen now binds 127.0.0.1 by default (--host flag opts in to 0.0.0.0); eliminates LAN exposure entirely. (2) CSRF guard in src/server/middleware.ts: state-changing requests (PUT/PATCH/DELETE/POST) are rejected if Origin/Referer does not match a loopback host; closes cross-site write vector from any website open while the dashboard is running. (3) CORS: wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* removed; handleCors() now reflects loopback origins only (sendJson updated). (4) New src/server/safe-path.ts (safeChildPath) guards /api/core/:filename against path-traversal; same URL-normalization defense applied in src/server/routes/core.ts. Also: npm audit fix eliminates picomatch ReDoS advisory (0 vulnerabilities). 8 new tests in tests/unit/server-security.test.ts, all green. Independent security review: PASS, no bypass found. Residual per-route traversal hardening for tasks/knowledge/features tracked in v06 (already gated by CSRF + loopback + URL-normalization defenses).
summary: v0.5.1: harden dashboard server — loopback bind, CSRF guard, CORS lockdown, path-traversal guard, dep audit

description: Two additions shipped together. (1) goal-skill skill-pack: subagent-driven goal completion orchestrator. Main agent dispatches goal-planner (opus) for a file-by-file plan, then 2 parallel goal-plan-reviewers iterate until SOLID (cap 3, then escalate), persists plan as a dreamcontext task, dispatches goal-implementer, runs reviewer until PASS (cap 3), then goal-validator against user-agreed criteria (unit/integration tests or manual checklist). Ships 4 sub-agents: goal-planner (opus), goal-plan-reviewer, goal-implementer, goal-validator. Reuses existing reviewer agent for code review. Trigger-only description with Red Flags + rationalization tables to prevent premature short-circuiting of convergence loops. (2) Related-skills recall hook injected into UserPromptSubmit: surfaces installed skills matching the prompt via BM25 (loadSkillDocs() corpus loader, top-level SKILL.md only, excludes alwaysApply packs), instructs agent to invoke matching skills via Skill tool BEFORE acting; self-contained never-throws block; DREAMCONTEXT_SKILLS_HOOK=0 opt-out. Gate redesigned in same session: no longer pre-lists a top-3 subset — tells agent to review the FULL skill catalog already in context and decide. 13 new test specs (7 unit recall-skill-corpus + 6 integration hook) green.
summary: Add goal-skill orchestration pack (plan→review→implement→validate loop) and Related-skills recall hook

description: Add three workstreams shipped together as v0.5.0. WS1 positioning: 'persistent brain — remembers, knows, learning to act' one-liner locked into 0.soul.md, knowledge/positioning.md, package.json description, and README; 'autonomous' banned (tested-absent) via positioning-doc.test.ts. WS2 one-command install: install.sh (POSIX, Node>=18 gate, no sudo/eval/nested-pipe, idempotent, non-TTY safe) ships in the npm tarball; curl|sh installs or updates based on presence of _dream_context/. WS3 in-session update nudge: dreamcontext upgrade [--check], pure src/lib/version-check.ts; non-blocking SessionStart nudge reads a cached check only — the single npm network call runs >=once/24h from UserPromptSubmit hook, never in the snapshot hot path; fail-safe (try/catch -> silent), opt-out via DREAMCONTEXT_VERSION_CHECK=0. Also fixes hardcoded CLI .version('0.1.0') -> dreamcontextVersion(); bumps package 0.2.0 -> 0.5.0. 63 new test specs pass.
summary: v0.5.0: lock 'persistent brain — remembers, knows, learning to act' positioning; one-command curl|sh install; in-session update nudge

description: Add business-idea-discovery and business-idea-validation skill packs under skill-packs/ for npm distribution. business-idea-discovery/SKILL.md: structured ideation framework. business-idea-validation/SKILL.md: 9-stage sequential validation pipeline (problem discovery, customer analysis, market sizing, competitive intel, simulated validation, synthesis, GTM, financials, go/no-go) with 6 specialist sub-agents (biv-researcher, biv-customer-analyst, biv-market-analyst, biv-financial-analyst, biv-strategist, biv-decision-gate) and kill gates between stages. Each stage produces a structured artifact building a cumulative dossier that ends in a scored GO/PIVOT/NO-GO verdict. catalog.json updated with both pack entries.

Replace multi-query BM25 keyword extraction with a single claude CLI call (Haiku model) for memory recall. src/lib/recall-query-extractor.ts completely rewritten: haikuRecall() builds a corpus index (capped at 8,000 chars), embeds it in a system prompt, calls claude --model haiku -p with no tools and no session persistence, and parses a JSON response of 0-3 doc keys. Falls back silently to raw BM25 when the claude CLI is not available (null return). src/lib/recall-multi-query.ts deleted. hook.ts updated to call haikuRecall() instead of the old multi-query path. Key design choices: execFileSync over SDK to avoid ANTHROPIC_API_KEY dependency (claude CLI uses its own auth); single call over multi-query because Haiku sees the full corpus index and understands intent cross-language (Turkish/English mixed prompts work correctly); AbortController + clearTimeout for clean timeout on the CLI path. Security and edge-case reviews applied; 4 findings fixed. 66 tests pass.

Remove coordinator sub-agent from multi-review skill; main agent synthesizes specialist reports directly. Coordinator added latency and token cost without clear value — the main agent already receives all specialist reports and has full diff context to dedupe and re-rank findings. Deleted skill-packs/agents/review-coordinator.md, simplified SKILL.md flow, updated REVIEWER_SHARED.md rubric, catalog.json, and knowledge/multi-reviewer-pattern.md to reflect the new direct-synthesis architecture. 8 files changed.

Update cross-references for multi-review skill. Added multi-review to AGENTS.md sub-agent roster table. Added Relationship sections to knowledge/sub-agent-iterative-reviewer-pattern.md and knowledge/three-reviewer-parallel-mandates-pattern.md distinguishing those pre-implementation adversarial patterns from the new post-implementation /multi-review diff-review skill.

Add /multi-review skill — router + 4 niche specialists + coordinator. Shipped 8 new files: .claude/skills/multi-review/SKILL.md (orchestration entry, fires on /multi-review), REVIEWER_SHARED.md (shared severity rubric + output format + what-not-to-flag rules), and 6 agents — review-router.md (classifies tier Trivial/Lite/Full and emits JSON dispatch plan), review-security.md (secrets, auth, injection, env leaks, weak crypto), review-cloud-functions.md (idempotency, infinite loops, billing footguns, trigger-loop hazards), review-frontend.md (file size, hooks, a11y, design tokens, XSS sinks), review-edge-cases.md (null/empty, concurrency, partial fails, retries — default-on at tier >= Lite), review-coordinator.md (only reader of full specialist reports; dedupes, re-ranks, emits READY_TO_MERGE / NEEDS_ATTENTION / NEEDS_WORK verdict). Each specialist declares dreamcontext skills in YAML frontmatter and loads them before reviewing, making this skill-aware in a way that generic multi-agent review frameworks are not.

summary: Cross-repo audit: align all skill/agent/template/doc surfaces with today's memory+changelog+snapshot+anti-bloat changes
description: All v0.4.0+ documentation surfaces synced to new reality (memory recall + CHANGELOG schema + tiered snapshot + default-on hook + 150-line cap). 5 parallel cluster agents updated: skill/SKILL.md (1 fix), agents/dreamcontext-{explore,initializer}.md (5 edits), .codex/ mirrors (5 prompts + 5 toml fully rewritten), src/templates/{CLAUDE.md,AGENTS.md,init/2.memory.md} (template-level fixes for new projects), root README+DEEP-DIVE+AGENTS+CHANGELOG.md, feature PRDs memory-recall-bm25 + context-snapshot + sleep-consolidation. Final stale-reference grep clean. Build green (555.12 KB).

Add saved graph views to Brain page. Users can name, save, and restore combinations of filter state in the Brain graph. View definitions are persisted to localStorage (key: dreamcontext:brain-saved-views). A badge on the Settings panel shows the count of saved views. Tested against headless Chromium, Brave binary, and Brave with user's full profile — localStorage write/read cycle verified stable. Bug surface during fix: Brave Incognito blocks localStorage by default (by design, not a code bug).

Fix mermaid rendering not working in MarkdownPreview and theme mismatch in TaskDetailPanel. Root causes: (1) MarkdownPreview had no mermaid rendering at all — flowcharts in Knowledge, Features, and Core pages rendered as raw <pre><code> blocks. (2) TaskDetailPanel's ensureMermaid() read prefers-color-scheme (OS theme) while the site uses data-theme; mermaid diagrams rendered in wrong color mode. (3) TaskDetailPanel.css had @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) rules that applied light styles even on dark-mode site. Fix: extracted a shared dashboard/src/lib/mermaidRender.ts module (useMermaidRender hook, initMermaid(theme), renderMermaidIn(root, idPrefix)) with ClickUp-styled themeVariables (Plus Jakarta Sans, deep-violet primary, theme-aware background). MarkdownPreview now uses useTheme().resolved via the shared hook. TaskDetailPanel drops ensureMermaid, calls initMermaid(theme), adds theme to effect deps — re-themes cleanly without duplicating wrappers. TaskDetailPanel.css replaces all prefers-color-scheme media queries with CSS variables (--color-bg-secondary, --color-border, --glass-bg). MarkdownPreview.css gains .mermaid-rendered wrapper, stage, and error styles. Semantic classDef overrides (done=green, active=yellow, todo=grey, blocked=red) in user content are preserved. Validated with Playwright: 1 wrapper, 1 SVG stage, 0 raw code blocks, 0 errors in both light and dark mode.

Add 2D/3D toggle to Brain page with lazy-loaded Three.js chunk. New dashboard/src/components/brain/BrainCanvas3D.tsx renders the knowledge graph using react-force-graph-3d with custom force configuration (charge, link, center, collide, forceX/Y/Z), billboard Three.js Sprite labels that always face camera (dimmed when node is not highlighted), scene fog for depth perception, and click-to-focus camera fly-to animation. BrainSettings.tsx gains a 'View' segmented control (2D | 3D) at the top of the Display section, backed by display.view: '2d' | '3d' in useGraphSettings.ts (default 2d). BrainPage.tsx imports BrainCanvas3D via React.lazy so the Three.js bundle (~334 KB gzip) is loaded on demand only when the user switches to 3D — the main index chunk stays at its pre-3D size. All existing Brain features (filtering, neighbor highlighting, click-to-drawer, color rules, hover-dim) work identically in 3D. New task file: _dream_context/state/brain-3d-view.md.
